source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The figure below shows a ring made of six small sections which you are to paint on a wall. You have four paint colors available and will paint each of the six sections a solid color. Find the number of ways you can choose to paint each of the six sections if no two adjacent section can be painted with the same color.

<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'The figure shows a ring made of six small sections', so the spatial arrangement is introduced through the figure
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate nontrivial visual reliance in the upstream record
the adjacency structure is a 6-section ring/cycle, which can be partly inferred from text, so dependence is high but not maximal
multi_step: solving requires modeling the sections as a cyclic adjacency graph and enforcing 'no two adjacent sections are painted the same color'
the task is a constrained counting/combinatorics problem rather than a direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='732', so the target output is precise
the combinatorial count can be independently checked by formula or enumeration for a 6-cycle with 4 colors
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and answer_manifest.answer_present=true support reliable downstream verification

risk_flags: figure topology may be inferable from the text alone, limiting strict image dependence
possible ambiguity about whether rotationally equivalent paintings are considered distinct

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和主图像均存在，资产完整且注册校验通过，没有显式缺失问题
初步评分整体较高：图像依赖度较高、多步推理需求明确、答案可验证性很强
风险主要是图形拓扑部分可由文字部分推断，以及旋转等价是否区分可能存在表述歧义，但这些不足以削弱样本的核心语义支撑

normalized_value.rewritten_question_text: The figure shows a ring made of six small sections which you are to paint on a wall. You have four paint colors available and will paint each of the six sections a solid color. agent_assessment.rationale: The question clearly asks for the number of valid colorings of a six-section ring with four colors and no equal adjacent sections. The image clearly shows the six adjacent ring sections needed to interpret the adjacency structure, and the expected answer format is a single numeric count.
